title: KRS - Marketing Hub Update
summary:
The meeting reviewed a new front-and-back annuities one-pager and connected it to broader marketing and lead-nurturing playbooks. Allyson presented the one-pager covering retirees’ concerns, annuity basics, benefits, and myth-busting and solicited field feedback to tailor content—suggestions included visualizing long‑term care riders, emphasizing product personalization and variety, and expanding similar one-pagers for life insurance and living benefits to reach younger markets.

Discussion then turned to distribution and campaign strategy: the team recommended embedding flyers in email and using the materials within multi-month nurture sequences rather than one-off sends, with attention to opt-out risk and identifying engaged leads. Kevin Beeftink introduced a channel-evaluation framework that converts annual income to hourly value and calculates required leads, appointments, and policies; his example showed $100,000/year (~$60/hour), a $500 average commission, needing 17 policies/month, ~68 appointments, and ~200 leads at a 35% meeting conversion rate. Kevin also compared cold-calling time costs to higher-priced “hot” leads, reviewed organic content and a systemized T65 mailer approach, and committed to sharing his spreadsheet and T65 enrollment data. The group reinforced using the Notion marketing hub, promoting documented playbooks (door-knocking, cold-calling, seminar reactivation), and agreed to convert meeting takeaways into concise, operational tactics; Allyson committed to revising and distributing the flyer and developing additional assets.

Chris Adkins will provide input on visualizing long-term care riders for the flyer
Allyson Rose Powers will expand the one-pager to emphasize long-term care riders and product personalization based on field feedback
Allyson Rose Powers will include financial literacy content in Client for Life communications for April
Kevin Beeftink and Allyson Rose Powers will discuss integrating the one-pager into a broader lead-nurturing campaign and target lists
Allyson Rose Powers will review and repurpose existing long-term care materials from Notion for the new flyer
Kevin Beeftink will share the spreadsheet used for the hourly valuation and lead-cost calculations with the team
Andres Sanchez will coordinate a follow-up meeting to review T65 metrics and share region-specific questions for discussion
Kevin Beeftink will assemble structured numbers and review enrolled T65 participants to prepare a case study and metrics presentation
Kevin Beeftink will investigate and report on logistic issues for mailers, including bounced addresses, for enrolled T65 campaigns
Agents will identify five current clients without annuities and call them to schedule appointments using the annuity flyer.
Allyson Rose Powers will revise the annuity flyer based on feedback and distribute it to the group.
Allyson Rose Powers will develop additional marketing flyers based on the feedback received.
Kevin Beeftink will continue to surface and reference documented playbooks (interviews and written playbooks) in future calls.
